mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >55% of periods following a 10bps FEDFUNDS increase, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces the Federal Reserve to pause rate hikes, triggering a short-term equity rally over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 3.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and accelerating inflation indicate demand destruction amid price shocks, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL acceleration fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45% or Rank IC consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns during recovery phases, with mean returns below zero and a t-statistic p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates restrictive monetary policy that overshoots equilibrium, causing liquidity contraction and negative equity returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized growth by >1.5%
failure_condition: The policy gap metric shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 48% and rolling IC remaining statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.3 million units signals collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, which precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 1.3 million fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >65% of triggered months and IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production outpaces GDP growth indicates supply-side resilience that buffers against demand shocks, producing positive short-term equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The production-GDP divergence metric yields negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or walk-forward testing shows a negative mean IC across all quarters.
